title: Seiberg-Witten Theory — Why SUSY Makes YM Solvable

analysis.object: T810: Seiberg-Witten Theory — Why SUSY Makes YM Solvable depth analysis

n2_susy_ym.description: N=2 SYM: Yang-Mills with N=2 supersymmetry -- solved by Seiberg-Witten 1994

sw_curve.description: Seiberg-Witten curve: elliptic curve over Coulomb branch -- EML-2/EML-3

sw_mass_gap.description: N=2 SYM: mass gap exists. Monopole condensation = confinement.
sw_mass_gap.reason: Mass gap proved in SUSY case

susy_depth_reduction.description: SUSY forces holomorphicity: theory is EML-3 controlled. Without SUSY: EML-inf.

susy_breaking.description: Soft SUSY breaking: adds EML-2 mass terms to break SUSY while keeping gap

gap_persistence.description: Does mass gap persist under soft SUSY breaking? SW predicts yes for small breaking.

t810_theorem.description: T810: SUSY reduces YM depth from EML-inf to EML-3 via holomorphicity. Mass gap proved at EML-3. Soft SUSY breaking keeps gap (EML-2 perturbation of EML-3 system). Non-SUSY YM = EML-inf target. T810.
t810_theorem.reason: SUSY mechanism: holomorphicity = depth reduction EML-inf -> EML-3

analysis.theorem: T810: Seiberg-Witten Theory — Why SUSY Makes YM Solvable (S1089).

key_theorem: T810: Seiberg-Witten Theory — Why SUSY Makes YM Solvable (S1089).
